episodes.title: Studying Amish Theology: Primary Sources & The Creation of Archives
episodes.description:
As a scholar of religion, I can share only a small window into the world of theological writing and transmission through narrow windows: archives at the Ohio Amish Library and what I have learned in specific Ohio communities. 

No "expert" can give you anything more than the view from narrow windows, read through the lens of experience and interpretation (ethnography).

Read more:

Biesecker-Mast, G. J. (1999). Anxiety and assurance in the Amish atonement rhetorics of Daniel E. Mast and David J. Stutzman. Mennonite Quarterly Review, 73(3), 525-539.
Biesecker-Mast, G. J. (2004). The Word Made Flesh: The Skin of History in Yoderian Historiography. Fides et historia, 36(1), 53.
Fast, G., & Snyder, C. A. (2001). Biblical concordance of the Swiss Brethren, 1540. Pandora Press [ua].
Mast, G. J. (2012). Unser Leit... The Story of the Amish. Mennonite Quarterly Review, 86(2), 265-270.
Kingdom Calls podcast: https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/anabaptist-kingdom-calls-penn-dutch/id15844077
Menno Simon's writings: https://www.mennosimons.net/completewritings.html
Martyr's Mirror: https://www.gutenberg.org/ebooks/65855 
The earliest hymns of the Ausbund: https://archive.org/details/earliesthymnsofa0000unse

Silencing, Denial, & Blame: Signs that Your Church is Enabling the Abuser (Part 1)
episodes.description:
Website: https://rebekahmui.vt.domains/ 

Rebekah Mui recently completed a PhD in the Alliance for Social, Political, Ethical and Cultural Thought at Virginia Tech. Her research engages anarcho-pacifism and Anabaptism with historic and imperial, sexual, and gender-based violence using theological, ethnographic, and oral history methods. She has published in the Bandung Journal of the Global South, Ecumenical Trends and CBE International’s Priscilla Papers and worked with the Virginia Tech Center for the Humanities and Virginia Tech Prison Book Project.

Ep #2 Defunding "Women's Studies"? The Elites are Lying to Us
episodes.description:
"Can Embattled Women's Studies Programs" survive? A new article from Inside Higher Ed explores the politics of women, gender, and sexuality research. Here's my take on the central question behind WGS research.

Rebekah Mui is a PhD Candidate in social and cultural thought, religion and culture studies, and political theology.

episodes.title: Ep #1 Introducing the Dissertation Podcast, Exploring Anabaptist Political Theology
episodes.description: Filming outdoors in the sun and wind of South Carolina. This podcast will consist of 20-30 minute long deep dive and interview episodes inviting a community of practice to converse on questions of gender-based and sexual violence, secondary victimatization, ecclesial history, spirituality, and Anabaptist political theology.
